summary: Crystal‑clear Miller soundboard captures a stellar early‑1990 spring run highlighted by High Time, Loose Lucy and a fire‑filled second set.
review: The recording is an excellent Miller SBD that many fans call the best source for this era – crisp, well‑balanced and free of major glitches. Musically the show shines with standout performances of High Time, Loose Lucy, The Weight, Cumberland Blues and a powerhouse second‑set sequence (Foolish Heart → Hey Pocky Way → The Other One / Wharf Rat / Good Lovin'). Overall reviewers rave about the band’s intensity and the quirky, well‑crafted setlist.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4–5) and enthusiastic comments about both performance and Miller soundboard; only a few mixed/low scores, so overall rating sits in the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller board praised as the best source – clear, crisp mix with minimal bleed; some versions edited but overall very listenable. No major drop‑outs or muffiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A quirky, well‑balanced flow: a ballad‑heavy first half (Easy to Love You, High Time, Queen Jane) that builds into upbeat numbers (Loose Lucy, Cassidy, Deal), followed by an adventurous second set with varied vibes and a high‑energy climax around Foolish Heart → Hey Pocky Way and the The Other One / Wharf Rat / Good Lovin' sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his intense, soaring leads on Cumberland Blues and throughout the second set; praised for “mad” solos and vocal mutters in the encore.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for taking reins on Cumberland Blues and delivering solid bass work during the second‑set peak.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for vocal delivery on ballads (Easy to Love You, High Time, Queen Jane) and his spacey contributions later in the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent praised repeatedly – “sounds great”, “purest and most honest” keyboard work throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann receives shout‑outs for an Appalachian feel on Cumberland Blues and solid drumming overall.
